articles.title: England look to Arsenal's playbook - Tuchel's set-piece plan
articles.summary: Arsenal are set-piece masters, with prominent figures in England's World Cup squad, so it is understandable that Thomas Tuchel will look to play to the Premier League champions' strengths.

articles.summary: Vinicius Jr scores a wonder goal to rescue his side but Brazil's performance against Morocco raises more questions than answers.

articles.summary: It might have been nervy. It might not have been pretty. But Scotland are here. And Scotland have won. Tom English reports from Boston.

articles.summary: Watch how Ivory Coast's Amad Diallo wins their Group E match against Ecuador in the 90th minute at the 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: What is 'Snicko' and how does it work at the World Cup?
articles.summary: BBC Sport's Ask Me Anything team explains what football's version of the Snickometer is and how it works after Mattias Svanberg's goal for Sweden against Tunisia at the World Cup.

articles.title: Summerville scores 'sizzler' as Netherlands retake lead
articles.summary: Crysencio Summerville's "sizzler" of a goal helps Netherlands retake the lead against Japan in their Group F fixture at the 2026 World Cup.

articles.title: Comenencia scores Curacao's first-ever World Cup goal
articles.summary: Livano Comenencia makes national history scoring Curacao's first-ever World Cup goal against Germany in their Group E match in Houston.

articles.title: McGinn fires Scotland to first World Cup win for 36 years
articles.summary: John McGinn's goal was enough as Scotland beat Haiti 1-0 at the Boston Stadium to record their first win at a World Cup for 36 years.

articles.summary: Watch highlights as Australia beat Turkey in their opening match, with 20-year-old Nestory Irankunda becoming the Socceroos's youngest ever World Cup goalscorer.

articles.title: 'I'll be staying out of the way' - Southgate on World Cup punditry
articles.summary: Former England manager Sir Gareth Southgate says he turned down work as a pundit at the World Cup because he did not think it would be "helpful" to the side's chances.

articles.title: Refugee who quit Bayern to create Aussie World Cup dream
articles.summary: Nestory Irankunda was born as a refugee in Tanzania before becoming Australian record breaker with goal in victory over Turkey.

articles.title: Why can't World Cup games kick off on time?
articles.summary: A delay to Scotland's Group C tie with Haiti in Massachusetts continued a noticeable trend of tardiness at the 2026 tournament, with none of the first eight matches kicking off on time.
